title: Lesson Quiz: Interest, Fees, and Cost of Borrowing
description: Assessment of the concept that How interest accumulates and affects total repayment.

choices.feedback: Correct. Interest is an added borrowing cost paid on top of the original amount.
The original amount borrowed is separate from the extra cost called interest.
Interest is a borrowing cost, not a reward.
Interest in this lesson refers to borrowing cost, not a savings deposit.

questions.prompt: Fees can increase the total amount a borrower must repay.

choices.feedback: Fees do add cost and can increase the total repayment amount.
Correct. Fees are part of the total borrowing cost.

questions.prompt: A borrower takes a $450 loan with $36 in interest and a $9 fee. What is the total repayment amount?

choices.feedback: Add the borrowed amount, interest, and fee carefully.
This total is too high for the loan, interest, and fee given.
That total is much higher than the correct repayment amount.

questions.prompt: Why is total repayment a stronger comparison than the borrowed amount alone?

choices.text: Because the borrowed amount already includes all later costs
choices.feedback: The borrowed amount does not automatically include all interest and fees.

choices.text: Because only the borrowed amount matters when comparing loans
choices.feedback: The borrowed amount alone is not enough. Interest and fees affect the real cost.

choices.text: Because fees and interest never matter in comparisons
choices.feedback: Fees and interest do matter because they affect total cost.

choices.text: Because total repayment shows the real cost after interest and fees are added
choices.feedback: Correct. Strong borrowing comparisons should use the full cost, not only the starting amount.

questions.prompt: A lower starting loan amount is always the cheaper borrowing option.

choices.feedback: A lower starting amount is not always cheaper if the interest and fees are higher.
Correct. The full borrowing cost should be compared before deciding which option is cheaper.
